what_it_does: Lists a batch of six source files or components under the claude-ds project, each with its relative path and shared root directory, as an allocation record for wave-002 interpretation.
observations: All entries share project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'.
Paths cluster in two folders: 'atomicity/' (two JSX components) and 'axes/' (one core JS file plus color, depth, and fill axis modules).
The file is a JSON array and contains no embedded code symbols or metadata beyond project/path/root triplets.
standouts: Mix of React JSX components (ViConsole.jsx, VoiceSurface.jsx) and axis subsystem JavaScript modules.
Tight thematic grouping around atomicity and axes.
conventions: Uses a consistent project/path/root triplet for each allocation entry.
Follows a JSON array batch format matching a ledger allocation file path.
concerns: No version, timestamp, or batch-purpose metadata is present.
No explicit rationale for why these six files were selected together.
notes: This is a wave-002 allocation batch that simply enumerates six claude-ds files for downstream interpretation.
questions: What criteria selected these specific files for batch-019?
Are the 'atomicity' and 'axes' groups intended to be interpreted together or as separate concerns?
purpose_vs_actual: The path indicates it is an allocation batch for wave-002 interpretation, and the content matches that purpose by listing files selected for processing.
intention_signals: It appears to catalog a focused slice of the claude-ds design system—voice/atomic UI surfaces and visual axes—so the one-system interpreter can process them as a coherent batch.
contribution: Provides a scoped, project-rooted file inventory that a fused system can use as a reliable source-of-truth for which paths belong to a given interpretation batch.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six claude-ds design-system files across atomicity components and axes modules for wave-002 interpretation.
intention_for_embedding: To record which files were selected for batch interpretation in the one-system ledger.
